importantVotes.title: 1939 Market Street Affordable Housing — $47.6M State Grant
importantVotes.description: Authorizes the Mayor's Office of Housing to execute agreements for a $47.6M California state grant supporting a 100% affordable housing project at 1939 Market Street, including a $35M loan to the developer and $12.6M to the city for infrastructure. Fielder, Melgar, and Sauter were excused.

importantVotes.title: Annual Surveillance Report — Gunshot Detection Technology
importantVotes.description: Approves the Department of Emergency Management's annual surveillance report on gunshot detection technology, as required by the city's surveillance oversight ordinance. Fielder, Melgar, and Sauter were excused.

importantVotes.title: Police Commission Statement of Purpose
importantVotes.description: Approves the Police Commission's formal Statement of Purpose as required under City Charter Section 4.102(2), defining the Commission's oversight role over the SFPD. Fielder, Melgar, and Sauter were excused.

importantVotes.title: Allied Universal SF General Hospital Security — $15.4M
importantVotes.description: Extends the unarmed security contract at San Francisco General Hospital by six months through December 2026 and increases the total contract value to $15.38 million. Fielder, Melgar, and Sauter were excused.

importantVotes.title: JPMorgan Chase StopScamsSF Grant — $3M
importantVotes.description: Retroactively authorizes the Office of Treasurer and Tax Collector to accept a $3 million grant from JPMorgan Chase to fund the StopScamsSF program, which helps residents avoid financial fraud and scams, running through December 2028. Fielder, Melgar, and Sauter were excused.

importantVotes.title: Landmark Designation Initiated — Engine Company No. 33
importantVotes.description: Initiates the historic landmark designation process for Engine Company No. 33 at 117 Broad Street, a historic firehouse in District 11. Fielder, Melgar, and Sauter were excused.
